name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Ride with Rouleur
description:
The weekly ride with the Rouleur Team. Each week we'll take on a 75km loop with the aim of completeing it as close to 2 hrs as we can.  Top marks for those who cross the line at exactly 2 hrs.  For most riders that will mean riding at between 2.5w/kg and 3 w/kg.  Stay as a group and it will be much easier.

Look out for notifications on our Instagram @rouleurmagazine for information about the ride!  Join the Rouleur club on Zwift.

name: Profitterolls p/b AUCC
description:
Welcome to Profitterolls! This isn't just a ride; it's a fun and faster (C-Pace) format designed to help you improve your fitness, speed, and communication skills. Each week, we'll choose from exciting formats like:

🔴 Wattbomb KOMs
🟠 Progressive Pace
🟢 Confetti Sprints

Both Yellow & Red Beacons are central to the action. An easier option is usually available with one of our beacons if you prefer a more stable ride to enjoy the lively atmosphere. You can join or rejoin this event for up to 30 minutes.

name: GGCC Glasgow Green Cycle Club Thursday Training Ride
description:
GGCC group rides are TRAINING events, category C, with a yellow beacon leader. The beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average wherever possible.

Please look out for our training rides 3 days a week, every week - Tuesdays at 20.45 UTC, Thursdays at 21.00 UTC and Saturdays at 11.30am UTC. Use #ggcc to search, as in https://zwift.com/events/tag/ggcc

The Glasgow Green Cycle Club (GGCC) training rides are intended to provide a challenge for riders, and are open to all timezones. You should ride as a group, following the instructions of the beacon ride leader(s) for any sprints, intervals and spicy segments, regrouping after each when requested. All our group rides also have a closing minirace, to start from behind the beacon.

GGCC is a Glasgow based cycling club whose “in real life” (irl) club runs and training rides are always organized into pace groups, which match the Zwift categories quite well. GGCC also has a good race pedigree and presence in Scotland, and so we have organized this training ride series to suit what we believe is relevant to bike riders’ interests in performance and improvement in our club, in other Glasgow clubs, in Scotland and the UK. Racers will, no doubt, aspire to try our other events, both our Monday 20.10 UTC Zwift races, and our Friday 19.45 UTC TTs.

As we expect our club’s riders to be able to ride 50 miles at a 15mph average on our Sunday rides, such steady riding members will be comfortable with Zwift Category C, and in our various rides we cater for all abilities, through the Zwift ABCD levels. Beacon leaders communicate via Zwift messaging (always) and/or Discord (sometimes) to vary the pace to allow for sprints, intervals, and optionally a mini-race towards the end of the rides, with regrouping after each segment. Make as much use of drafting as you can, for more effective sprinting and intervals.

Results can be seen at the end of the ride on screen, and we will, in common with many other ride series, publish your results on zwiftpower.com too, based on each category.

All bikes and PUs are allowed, but better not to use TT bikes (which don’t draft) on our group rides and races. Please add GGCC (X) to your Zwift name to allow your ride presence to be recognized, where X is your desired ride category. Categories are related to your Functional Threshold Power (FTP); use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg, then pick the correct ride and group.

C: 2 W/kg to 3 W/kg FTP; the beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average.

PLEASE NOTE: Strava data for our rides must be open (not private or hidden). Please use a heart monitor so that we can monitor correlated power and heart rate to help assure fair riding.
You can use our Discord channel to talk to each other at https://discord.gg/UDhdEQA
Please see the GGCC Zwift page link at http://www.glasgowgreencycleclub.co.uk/ for more.

For more about specific Zwift courses for our events, see the Zwift Companion schedule, and also https://zwiftinsider.com/ where there is a wealth of information about all things Zwift.

#ggcc

eventSubgroups.name: GGCC Glasgow Green Cycle Club Thursday Training Ride (C)

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter
accessExpression:
(profile.playerType == "BROADCASTER") ||
(subgroup.label == 1 && scoring.current >= 390 && scoring.current <= 650) ||
(subgroup.label == 2 && scoring.current >= 270 && scoring.current < 510) ||
(subgroup.label == 3 && scoring.current >= 160 && scoring.current < 390) ||
(subgroup.label == 4 && scoring.current < 270) ||
(subgroup.label == 5 && scoring.current < 160)

name: ZSUN vFUN Social Ride - 1.7-1.9 w/kg
description:
Join ZSUN for our weekly vFUN Social Ride – a relaxed, supportive event perfect for spinning into the weekend. This steady-paced ride (1.7–1.9 w/kg) is all about good vibes, easy chat, and enjoying the ride with fellow Zwifters.

☀️
Expect laughs, friendships, and plenty of encouragement. The yellow beacon will hold to the advertised pace, no matter the course. Wear the iconic #redshorts and feel part of the team!

🗓️
Weekly | Friendly chat encouraged | Everyone welcome

🎧
Join Discord to chat or just listen in

🎁
Complete the ride to earn your ZSUN kit

Put on your favourite playlist, spin those legs, and celebrate cycling in a fun, inclusive space.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN vFUN Social Ride - 1.7-1.9 w/kg (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a weekly, social event hosted by ZSUN.
☀️ 🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♂️🚴🏼‍♂️🚴🏼🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️ 🦄

Join us for a supportive group ride where you will get to wear our legendary #redshorts. Everything is easier with friends and the distraction of easy chatting! Yellow beacon will ride at advertised pace, so don’t ever let the course put you off. The main goal is to have a laugh and make new friends from all over the world. BTW, this is a great way to get ready for your weekend!

Put your music on (and your fan), spin those pedals and celebrate being on your bike in a safe and happy space! Everyone is welcome ☀️

name: Black Sheep Black Friday Workout
description:
The BLACK FRIDAY WORKOUT

It’s always time for a workout ride somewhere! So we are flipping Black Friday on its head. We are Aussies, after all. The Black Friday will be a morning workout for locals. A night before workout for Euro’s and somewhere in between for our friends in America.

Black Friday Workout is a pure social workout at its best. We usually end with a beer, so let’s make it a coffee. And hey, why don’t we throw in a new kit as well. Finish the workout to unlock your very own Black Sheep Racing Club kit in-game.

Join us for the Black Friday Workout each Friday at 6:30 am AEST.

name: WMZ Sugar & Spice
description:
Our Leadership Team consist of Andy,Joker,Ian

The start of race lap we Must the Behind Yellow or DQ
The race lap will be with the yellow beacon, who will win the cup ?

If you want to help as sweeps to hold the group together, you can tell us and we will look into helping you to help the Team

Every Friday of Australia Oceania Morning

Those who have never been here always Welcome to join our great Team. We ‘re waiting for you all

On Behalf of the Team, Thank you

description:

We will aim for average 1.8 wkg.

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 36
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #367

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Restart Esports - Thursday Thumper
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

eventSubgroups.name: Restart Esports - Thursday Thumper (A)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join in on a new weekly race: the Restart Racing Thursday Thumper.

Restart Racing is an eRacing team that likes to race hard and have fun. eSports gives us an amazing platform to better ourselves as bike racers and is a great way to make friends and enjoy ourselves. 

Each week will feature a different course ranging in distance from 45-60kms with a target finish time around 1.5hrs. Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.

Cats A & B riders require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) plus a heart rate monitor. Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual record and make analysis 'Public' on Zwift Power while using a direct drive trainer and power meter.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.

Cat A - 4.0+ (min 250W)
Cat B - 3.2 - 4.0 w/kg (min 200W)
Cat C - 2.5 - 3.2 w/kg (min 150W)
Cat D - 1.0 - 2.5 w/kg

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Move for Lex
description:
The Move for Lex workout honors avid Zwifter and cyclist Alexa Leary, who was seriously injured in a bike crash in July 2021. 

Lex was one of Australia’s top emerging junior triathletes at the time of her accident and is now facing months of rehabilitation. 

The #moveforlex movement is all about moving for those who can't and we'd love to see a huge group moving for Lex here every Friday for a workout.

For more information on the Move For Lex movement visit https://www.moveforlex.com/

name: JZQc Entrainement de Groupe
description: Divers entraînements chaque semaine afin d’améliorer votre endurance !

name: AATC Thursday Interval Workout
description:
WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Dana of the Ann Arbor Triathlon Club - Group workouts make training so much more fun. The Ann Arbor Triathlon Club promotes the sport of triathlon in Southeast Michigan. We have all levels of Triathletes.  

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. 

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

name: Faster Masters A/B/C Team Pursuit
description:
Thursdays’ Team Pursuit includes three teams starting at different times. Team C will start first followed by Team B, with Team A departing last. The goal is for the faster teams behind to catch Team C during the ride while all three teams hold their respective advertised paces. Starting time gaps may be adjusted during the season to maintain the ride concept, that being: as faster teams catch the team(s) in front, the catch will offer riders in a slower group who wish to increase their effort to do so by joining the faster team as they pass. Additionally, the catch offers riders in the faster team(s) the opportunity to jump in with the slower team(s) and recover to the finish. Riders who jump onto a faster team may realize they cannot hold the pace have an option to fall back to their starting group and try the same the next week to see if they can hold on longer. This is a great way to get stronger riding in a team format. 

All rides include a 30-minute Late Join period. During this time, dropped riders can use Late Join to rejoin their team. If you find that the team you selected is either too fast or too slow for your pace, you can switch teams within the first 30 minutes of the start time of the team you would like to join by using Late Join. 

We do not sweep during the first 30 minutes of a ride. If you fall behind for any reason during this time, we recommend using Late Join. We usually do not have designated sweepers (Red Beacon). After the first 30 minutes, if you get dropped and want to rejoin your team, please send a message in the format HELP(X), where X is the number of seconds you are off the back (for example, HELP5). If you reach out early, one or more stronger riders may come to assist you. If a sweep is needed we recommend you message help within 10 seconds.

We look forward to having you join us. The team pursuit is an excellent endurance and strength building adventure. 

Connect with Faster Masters on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2286721591625605

name: SWX Thursday Threshold by Coach Cindy 🇺🇸🇨🇦🇧🇷
description:
Push your limits every Thursday with SWX Threshold — a focused effort session built to improve your FTP and race performance.
Designed by Coach Cindy (Train Smart = Race Stronger), this session targets sustained high-intensity intervals just below or at threshold. It’s not easy, but it works. Bring your grit, legs, and a full bottle.

• All levels welcome
• Stay-together mode enabled
• Late join allowed

Connect and learn more about who we are:
📸 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shark_worx_swx
🌐 Website: https://bit.ly/Visit_Coach_Cindy

eventSubgroups.name: SWX Thursday Threshold by Coach Cindy 🇺🇸🇨🇦🇧🇷 (E)

name: RO4H "Aaahh, Thursday!" Social Ride
description:
Welcome to the RO4H “Aaahh, Thursday!” Social Ride.

The RO4H “Aaahh, Thursday!” Social Ride is a social group ride hosted by the Ride On 4 Health community. We foster a friendly, casual, supportive, and respectful atmosphere that embraces everyone from the most seasoned Zwifter to the first-time group ride participant.

The pace varies depending on the terrain, but the ride will have an overall average pace of around 18–20 MPH (27–32 KPH). The pace on the flats sections will be slightly faster in the peloton and be sub 2 W/KG on climbs. We typically ride mostly flat routes with only a few short hills, if any. Sometimes we take on some slightly hillier routes for variety, but most of our rides will be gently rolling to flat terrain.

We try to be a no-drop ride, but ultimately, this pace is a recovery pace for some and a great effort for others. You should be prepared to meet the average pace and strive to stay in the ride leader's peloton to benefit from the group draft effect. Being a social ride, we don’t chase flyers — but flyers are free to fly. The “Aaahh, Thursday!” Social Ride is certainly not a race.

Sweepers are available to assist those off the back. We do not want anyone to ride alone unless they choose. The sweepers will variably help riders bridge to the leader's peloton or form a second pace group. We do not want riders to ride alone unless they want to.

Join us for the RO4H “Aaahh, Thursday!” Social Ride —you'll almost certainly break a sweat, hear a few dad jokes, and might even have fun.

View the RO4H ride schedule: https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/ro4h

Look us up on FB: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZwiftRideOn4Health/

#ro4h

name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders Team as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run (E)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Phoenix Rising - KNIGHT RIDE (banded)
description:
Welcome to PHOENIX RISING KNIGHT RIDE. This is a 60-minute banded ride where the whole group will stick together as long as you are pedaling. We generally ride on average 1.5 WKG. Some may have a higher WKG up front pulling the band, you are welcome as well. Get a burn in those legs!!!!! We also have a sweep team at your disposal in case you fall out of the band. We are not racers but conquerors, an inclusive and dedicated group striving to help riders achieve their goals through teamwork, friendships and most of all unwavering support. Our rides are about the journey. We have a special bond with all those who join. We expect everyone to be treated with respect. Empathy and tolerance are appreciated as others will have differing views. Stay focused on what unites us: cycling and Zwift. Practice kindness. Please join our team every Thursday!

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/mNMva8ekJR
Check us out on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/615972979730125/

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - The Fan Flats
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EZR Take It EZ-er
description:
All riders are welcome to join the EZ Riders (EZR) for this relaxed pace social group ride. Whether you are new to Zwift or group rides, looking for a recovery/rehab ride, or just looking to ride with a supportive group of friends, this ride is for you. The ride leader will be staying within the advertised average pace of this ride as we explore Zwift’s various worlds. Please respect the ride leader’s pace and ride with the yellow beacon.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/kmfS4YN

Facebook Page: http://www.facebook.com/groups/zer.cycling

#zer #ezr #ezriders

eventSubgroups.name: EZR Take It EZ-er (D)

name: USMeS - Movement Drill (D) 1.8 - 2.2w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS Movement Drill - 1.8 - 2.2
This session is a step up and designed to prepare you for Force Development immediately following. The focus is on coordinated movement, controlled pressure, and working together while the intensity nudges a tad higher.
✅ Pace: an average of 1.8–2.2 w/kg
✅ Duration: 55 minutes
While we aim for an overall ~2.1w/kg average, ( 'ISH ), we do push on climbs, and sprints WILL be encouraged. When the pace lifts, make your move, then fall back in quickly and keep the group intact.
Ride sharp, respect the leader, and protect the group.
This is where we wake things up.
Move well now. Be ready to apply what you've learned, @Force Development, next.
#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted

name: Epic Run Crew: Thursday Thunder
description:
It’s nearing the end of the work week. Perhaps it’s time to pick up the pace and go for it? Bring on the thunderous feet!

This is a 45 minute group run. You are welcome to run at your own pace (AYOP). Feel free to continue running after you finish the 45 minutes, or end it early. It’s up to you.

Chatting is encouraged! Talk about food. Make a funny observation. Whatever. We don’t judge. Just have some fun!

Epic Run Crew is based in Vancouver, BC, Canada. We were founded as an in-person club in 2021 and then joined Zwift in June 2022.

Use the Zwift Companion app to join the Epic Run Crew club, for access to exclusive member-only group runs.

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/epicruncrew2/
Instagram: @epicruncrew

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 28
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #367

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Kinetic Group Ride
description: Join us for the Kinetic Group Ride -- all trainers welcome -- and sharpen your sprint skills on this disciplined social ride.  We will ride 50 minutes at a 2.5 pace with some sprints just for fun on laps 2, 4 and 5 of the London Classique Course.  We regroup after the sprints and use the fence to hold to the planned pace.  Feeling a bit edgy?  We have you covered with an optional after party when the ride ends at a faster pace for 10 minutes.  Keep up with news about the ride on the community page at:  https://www.facebook.com/groups/kinetic.riders/

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: The HERD Thirsty Thursday
description:
The Herd's Thirsty Thursday (THTT) Dual Pace Social Group Ride

This group ride offers a dual pace in a social environment. The goal is to keep our rides entertaining with games and trivia, educational with Zwift tips, and on pace. This ride offer our traditional Cat “D” option or a slightly higher paced Cat “C” option.

Our ride leaders’ goal is to keep to the advertised pace, averaged over the course of the ride. However, bear in mind that there are situations such as incline and descents that will affect the pace. To this, we ask that you pay attention to the rider leader announcements and adjust your pace accordingly. Stay close to the yellow beacon where the draft is most effective. As is customary to all Herd rides, we offer full Yellow (Ride Leader) and Red beacon (Sweeps) support.

If you find yourself wanting more of a challenge, we encourage you to join our sweep team as they ride “sweep-tervals” (above advertised pace) to help bring riders back to the ride leader.

Riding alone is not an option, as Herd rides are geared to be inclusive and fun.

#herd

name: KRG Thursday Night Crit
description:
While we are all stuck at home, please join us in the virtual edition of Fair Park Crits, a mainstay of DFW cycling.

We are NOT using standard Zwift categories, and also having a women's only race. If you are a usual nighter racer you know where to sign-up, but if you're joining us virtually:

As and Bs into the "A" category. This is a "P/1/2/3" field. 

Zwift Cs into "B". This is a "3/4" race. 

Ds and beginner racers please choose "C". AKA the 4/5 or novice race. 

Ladies, you can choose your correct field above or race the women's only, up to you! 

If you race the A race irl, you may find yourself not quite fitting into the pace of a Zwift A field. If that's the case do an FTP test, make sure your weight is up to date and let us know so we can look at revisiting the categories. 

For more info join us on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/fairparkcrits/

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Southern Coast Cruise
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: USMeS - Force Development - Early - 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS - Force Development - Early - 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
This is a spicier C ride designed to build power, test your legs, and keep the group together. Expect controlled intensity, climbs that push you, and spots to spice things up and make the ride more fun.
✅ Pace: an average of 2.2–2.8 w/kg
✅ Duration: 60 minutes
We aim for an overall ~2.5 w/kg average, we do push on climbs, and when the pace lifts, go for it, then fall back in quickly and ride as a unit.
Ride sharp. Respect the leader. Protect the group. This is where we develop force, together.
Push hard. Ride together.
#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted

name: Roll with Castelli - Weekly Social
description:
Castelli fans, join us for our weekly Castelli social ride. 

This is a no-drop banded social ride, so ride as hard or easy as you like, we'll roll together.

Ride duration is usually around 50 - 60 minutes.

Complete the ride to unlock one of several Castelli kits.

If you’re in need of motivating tunes, check out the Squadra Castelli playlist:
https://castellicycling.link/spotify

name: Bag that Badge Series
description:
Open to all runners, Bag that Badge series is hosted by Stephen Cousins and is a fun way to earn your Zwift route badges.
Stephen Cousins is an active member of the Zwift Runners Facebook group and founder of Film My Run (FMR)
Join the FMR Club here https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join
Watch the Live Stream of this event on the Zwift Run Channel at YouTube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

Route Schedule rotates weekly:
Handful of Gravel
Surfin’ Safari
Splash and Dash
11.1 Ocean Blvd
5k Loop
Chili Pepper
Jon’s Route
That’s Amore
Couch to Sky k
Flat irons
Hudson Roll
Park to Peak
May field
Mayan Bridge Loop
Italian Villas Circuit

name: Film My Run 500 with Noble Pro
description:
Join Stephen Cousins and NoblePro for a 500 metre climb. Set your treadmill to 12% incline and run 4.2km of distance to reach 500 metres elevation. If your treadmill doesn't go that high, set it as high as it will go and join us anyway!

The run will be progressive starting at a walking pace of 5kph rising to 7kph for the final kilometre. Get on to the balls of your toes and get your calf muscles to do the work. This continuous uphill effort will help build strength and endurance for those hilly trail runs

This event is sponsored by the ever popular Noble Pro treadmills. You can get a discount on a NoblePro SMART treadmill from this link https://zwift.run/noble-pro

You can watch the run on the live stream at http://youtube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

Join the FMR Run Club for more fun events at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 32
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #367

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: Fearless Flying Feet Track Intervals
description:
Ready to get your feet flying by running track intervals of 800 meters followed by a 400 meter recovery. FIVE total times to gain speed. Challenge yourself to get better with each interval and come back week after week!

"He who would learn to fly one day must first learn to stand and walk and run and climb and dance; one cannot fly into flying.” Friedrich Nietzsche
https://www.brainyquote.com/quotes/friedrich_nietzsche_159166

#fear

name: BMC/Walmart Cycling Club Social
description:
Join the BMC/Walmart Cycling Club for a spirited group ride on this frozen arctic-blast night! (For much of the USA, at least) This is a social ride, with some spice. It is all about fun. Feel free to fly but it is more fun to ride together. Beacon will maintain prescribed pace with the potential for a few spicy moments. Sweepers will be back. All will be keeping the good vibes strong!
The BMC / Walmart Cycling Team was born to promote fitness through cycling in NWA. We are a group of enthusiasts cyclists and professional racers participating in all the different disciplines cycling has to offer. BMC is a cycling club located in and around Northwest Arkansas formed in 1979 by among others, the late Joe Martin. We are roadies, mountain bikers, tandem riders, tri-athletes, unicyclists, etc. We ride bicycles and that’s our “community”. Visit http://www.ridebmc.com to learn more or to join the club!

name: ZLDR Mid-Week Long Run
description:
Welcome to the ZLDR 21.1k Mid-Week Long Run. Whether you come for 3k, 5k, 10k or the full distance we are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners who will do our best to help you reach your goals.

Whether you’re speeding across the roads of Makuri or taking a gentle jog, join us for whatever distance but keep joining us and we can help you get to 21.1k and beyond. It’s always easier to run with others.

Not everyone gets the weekend off. For those of us who give our lives over to work on the weekends, please join us every week for your mid-week long run!

We’ve got 21.1km (13.1 miles) set aside just for you! Run as little or as much as you’d like, feel free to run more, to work in your interval training, to ease in a few recovery miles…. Whatever your mid-week goal may be, we’re here to help make it possible!

As with any of our runs, you are not required to go the entire distance listed. But we are a distance running group and would like to offer our members a chance every week to put their legs to the test and see how far they can go.

Hope to see you there!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Strava:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: Stage 4: Pas Racing: Serpentine 8
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Southern Coast Cruise
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Bikealicious Thursday Spectacular
description: We are a friendly gathering of cycling enthusiasts. We ride for 90 minutes, and for no less than 20 miles. We ride a new route every Thursday to keep the workout fresh and fun for all the zwifters. We ride with a rubber band to motivate all skill sets to stay together through challenging climbs resulting in an interactive and dynamic workout. Our group includes riders of all power levels. We welcome all riders.

name: Fell Swoop Speed Series
description: This crit series is designed for those who want a short and fast race to keep the engine primed without over doing it ahead of the weekend activities.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - The Fan Flats
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: EVR Club Ride
description:
EVR Club Ride

#evr

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: USMeS - Force Development - Late - 2.2 - 2.8w/kg
description:
Welcome to USMeS - Force Development - Late - 2.2 - 2.8w/kg

This is a spicier C ride designed to build power, test your legs, and keep the group together. Expect controlled intensity, climbs that push you, and spots to spice things up and make the ride more fun.
✅ Pace: an average of 2.2–2.8 w/kg
✅ Duration: 60 minutes
We aim for an overall ~2.5 w/kg average, we do push on climbs, and when the pace lifts, go for it, then fall back in quickly and ride as a unit.
Ride sharp. Respect the leader. Protect the group. This is where we develop force, together.
Push hard. Ride together.
#conqueringlimits
#challengeaccepted
"

name: Cycle Nation Weekend Warmup with M&T
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Weekend Warmup with M&T. Join us for an easy morning ride to get ready for the weekend. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere.

Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.
Connect with Cycle Nation:
Visit our website: www.cyclenation.co.za

name: Team Italy Super Early Ride
description:
Welcome to our Team Italy Super Early Ride, if you are an European rider, this is the best way to begin your day, if you are riding from the other part of the world, this is a very nice way for finish it. This ride is C category, follow the leader instructions for the next 60 minutes and enjoy it, be careful because the draft is very strong, the fence is killer, so the ride leader can kick you out of the ride if you don't respect the pace.
Please add the Tag Ita after your surname, you'll unlock our jersey if you complete the ride. join our Team Italy club on Companion for all the events.
This is our Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly and Discord Channel of Team Italy: https://discord.gg/c7PGKGv
We have also a Strava Club Team Italy: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
#teamitaly #jerseyunlock #respect4leader

name: The Zombie 5k
description:
Brought to you from the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) this event is called the Zombie Run as for those in the UK this event is in the middle of the night.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 5k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, Tuesday to Saturday and each day there is a different course.

name: "Russian Step - Hamster Style"
description:
“Russian Step - Hamster Style” is a cadence game where your legs spin like a hamster wheel! Alternate between high-speed spins (100–120 RPM) and controlled strength efforts (60–75 RPM). It builds coordination, pedaling efficiency, and muscle activation. Stay focused and flow with the rhythm. Let’s spin like warriors!
The “Russian Step - Hamster Style” workout is perfect for those aiming to improve cadence agility, pedaling efficiency, and neuromuscular responsiveness. By alternating very high RPM efforts with slower, strength-focused segments, it trains your body to handle sudden changes in rhythm and builds muscular coordination. It’s an excellent session for riders of all levels who want to break routine, sharpen their pedal stroke, and have fun while training. It also keeps the mind engaged and legs responsive.
⸻

        •        Warm-up (10 min):
        •        Easy spin at 90 RPM, gradually increasing power to 60% FTP.
        •        Main set (45 min):
        •        6 x (6 min intervals):
        •        1 min @ 80% FTP, 65 RPM
        •        1 min @ 95% FTP, 90 RPM
        •        30 sec @ 110 RPM, 70% FTP
        •        30 sec @ 60 RPM, 90% FTP
        •        1 min @ 100 RPM, 75% FTP
        •        1 min recovery @ 50% FTP, 85 RPM
        •        Finale (5 min):
        •        Progressive cadence ramp: start at 85 RPM, increase 5 RPM every minute
        •        Cool-down (10 min):
        •        Easy spin at 60% FTP, 85 RPM

name: Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Joburg Sunrise ride. Join us for an easy morning coffee ride aimed at Zwifters looking for an easy paced morning group ride. All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere.

Please follow the ride leaders instructions and try group around the beacon. Most importantly this isn't a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.

name: Team Valhalla [TV] Friday Fun Ride into the Weekend
description:
🌄 The weekend is calling… and Valhalla awaits!
Join the warriors of Team Valhalla [TV] every Friday as we saddle up for an epic, social ride to kick off the weekend with power and purpose. Whether you're a battle-hardened Zwifter or just forging your path, this ride is all about fun, inclusion, and camaraderie.

🪓 Choose your clan:
D Pace Shieldmaidens & Shieldbearers – A steady cruise for recovery or newer riders
C Pace Berserkers – A stronger effort for those ready to hammer the hills together

Together, we ride in formation, keep the group tight, and motivate like true Vikings. Expect banter, encouragement, maybe a few horns sounding as we charge into the weekend.

💥 No battles, no drops – just a crew of legends making every watt count. Whether you're in it for the laughs, the training, or the Valhalla vibes – we’ve got a place for you in the pack.
Grab your gear, summon your inner warrior, and ride with us. The gates of the weekend are open – let’s storm them together!

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 4 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 4: Standing Skills
Time to focus on maintaining control when transitioning from seated to standing, building confidence for rollers, small rises, and quick changes in pace.

name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B
description:
Welcome to GXY BOOST!
This is a twice weekly interval event which is targeted at riders looking to develop strength and endurance.
The events are open to all riders not just category B , but also those moving from rides of lower intensity.
BOOST! rides will be generally focusing between Vo2 and Threshold so expect the average wkg to finish between the
advertised power range based on a 75kg rider .
The sessions will be between 60 -90 minutes duration depending on intensity, course chosen or ride leader.
There will be no red sweeping beacon, so if you drop from the group simply continue the workout at your own pace.
We will of course continue to support you throughout the ride, just hang on in there and try and make it through to the
end!
Feel free to join in the fun during the ride by using the messaging system or if you prefer our dedicated BOOST!
Discord channel.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed and we are of course looking forward to
riding with you.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link.
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B (B)

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.8 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: 3R SPARK Interval Ride [~2.2-2.5 w/kg avg]
description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event (not a race). 3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders is Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Wednesdays are a steady recovery ride, where we split into two different ability groups. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Rides are typically around an hour long. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Watopia Dawn Patrol
description:
Welcome to Watopia Dawn Patrol hosted by Oslo Dawn Patrol.

The Dawn Patrol rides are an early morning social workout spin originating in Oslo, Norway that has since been adopted by local riders in several cities across the country and Europe. While a core group of riders braces the elements outdoors all year round, many of us take to the indoors during the winter season, bringing Watopia Dawn Patrol to life.

We head out for a ~60 min pre-work spin around the event-only route The Magnificent 8. This is a social group ride averaging no more than 3 W/kg with a little race up the reverse Hilly KOM. Stop pedalling after you’ve finished the Zwift KOM to regroup, and we will ride the remainder of the route together.

LEADER'S PACE:
- 2.0 W/kg out of the starting pen (we recommend you warm up to that pace 5 min before the start)
- 2.5-2.7 W/kg on flat sections
- 2.7-3.0 W/kg on climbs

REGROUP:
We regroup after the descent following the Hilly KOM, between km 11-11.3 (before the left turn that takes us to the Fuego Flats). Come to a complete stop until the ride leader announces that we are ready to continue.

WANNA RIDE HARDER?
If you're feeling strong and want to ride harder, a fantastic way to do this with the group is by dropping back to lend a wheel to someone who has fallen off the back for any reason.

You are welcome to join our Discord for the ride: https://discord.gg/3tTtcUm

Please tag @oslodawnpatrol in your Instagram updates.

Information and photos from the ride: https://www.facebook.com/OsloCyclingProject/
